title: 7 Things Humility Actually Looks Like
caption:
Your culture gave you a broken definition of humility.

It made it soft. Passive. The thing a man does when he has no spine. That's not it.

Humility says "I was wrong" before he knows how it'll land. It takes the job nobody saw. It asks for help before it needs rescuing. It doesn't make every room about itself.

The humble man receives correction without deflecting, carries someone else's story like it matters, and gets on his knees — because he knows who he is before God. Seen, small, and loved.

That last part is what builds the kind of confidence nothing else can.

"Act justly, love mercy, and walk humbly with your God." — Micah 6:8

Save this and pick one for this week.
